title: Printing a Gantt chart
excerpt: Is there a way to print a large Gantt chart across multiple pages? The PDF option seems ineffective as it produces a single screenshot without properly formatting the chart across multiple pages. This functionality appea…

posts.author: jameswright (james)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there a way to print a large Gantt chart across multiple pages? The PDF option seems ineffective as it produces a single screenshot without properly formatting the chart across multiple pages. This functionality appears to be a basic feature, and while it used to exist based on information from YouTube, it seems to have been removed.

The last discussion on the topic was in April 22.

Thanks

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey James,

I hear where you’re coming from. At this stage, it isn’t currently supported to break up the gantt across multiple pages, I apologise for the setback

I will follow-up with our team internally about our plan to improve this moving forward!

posts.author: jameswright (james)
posts.cookedHtml: What would the priority of this being fixed with Monday.com? I would like a sense if this is a dream never to be solved or actually something we can depend on being addressed. Considering its been like this for what looks like at least 2 years I don’t feel optimistic.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey James,

I recognise that this has been a long standing request and followed up with our team about where this currently sits on our roadmap - I will get back to you with an answer as soon as I can!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: https://monday.slack.com/archives/CTH46N4RL/p1707798049388859

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey James, thanks for your patience with me!

I’ve heard back from our team internally who clarified that at this stage it isn’t on their immediate roadmap, however they do plan to tackle the gantt towards the end of the year, so your feedback is incredibly important. I understand this isn’t necessarily the response you were after but rest assured I will share updates with you as soon as they’re shared with me.

posts.author: jameswright (james)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bianca,

Thanks for the update. Can you advise is there is an Add-in that one could purchase that would solve this problem?

I would add that the export of RAW data needs to be improved. Currently the export is not in simple columns and rows that can be imported into other systems. When we import data into Monday.com you require simple columns and rows but when Monday.com provides export you don’t do the same. That is nuts.

Thanks

James

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I have investigated internally James and transparently, I am not familiar with a recommended app for this purpose. I will continue to keep an eye out and discuss with the team if they’re familiar with any apps. I appreciate your feedback, and have shared this with the team responsible for the this functionality

posts.author: nscaggiante (Nicola)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca, is there any news on this topic? We really need to be able to print large gantts with higher freedom, as of now they collapse in formats that are not ideal. Thanks!

posts.author: Hanael (Hanael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep, Gantt export capabilities are very frustrating. It’s barely usable if not simply unusable with long term (more than 2 years) and complex project such in urban planning, building industry and more.

This issue has been a no go more than a few times, we lost good deals as a Monday Partner.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Hanael @jameswright @nscaggiante, I built a monday board view app called GanttGo that handles Gantt chart exports to PDF, PowerPoint and Excel where u can export your raw data to. It would also help with printing the gantt chart on multliple pages. Happy to answer questions!

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would be a great addition, since monday.com doesn’t support printing a large Gantt across multiple pages - the PDF export is just a single screenshot.

Workaround we use export the Gantt to Excel, then use Copilot to format it into a one-page (or multi-page) PDF. Not ideal, but it does the job for now.

posts.author: Hanael (Hanael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just tested: nice! Exactly what I was looking for! Great job!
I was about to succeed with Vibe but no yet there and not as good.

You saved my day: I have a meeting this afternoon with architects on this specific subject.

The pro version has a fair price, which makes it easy to sell, as the “small add-on that does what it says”. The logo thing is great (no just a nice to have at least in the engineering and building industry)

My suggestions would be:

  • Allow to display element with no timeline
  • Allow to choose columns to display in the left hand panel (e.g duration in days, status, etc.)
  • Allow to hide task progress (XXX% tag)
  • On the export, allow to add a custom document title (e.g “Aera B3 - Planning Week 32”)
  • On the export, allow to add a footer (like a text field for company detail or else)
  • On the export, allow to add a custom note (to add some thing specific to this print copy, like a important note, versioning or else: very often needed)(in the header, upper part of the document)
  • On the export, allow to add the export date (e.g a checkbox)

=> Once this done, I built you a church or something

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Hanael thanks so much! really glad it helps!

Great suggestions- some of those already exist and the rest are on the way:

  • Items with no timeline- They show in “unscheduled” section below the chart
  • Custom document title- You can set it in the export dialog
  • Export date- already included in all exports
  • Choose column in left panel, hide progres % , custom footer, custom header note- love these ideas, working on them

I’ll keep you posted as they roll out and i’ll hold you to that church!

posts.author: Hanael (Hanael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Oups… my mistake. Too early, too quick, too enthusiastic…

A clarification regarding unscheduled elements: I would expect them in their group (after the preceding element?) and in the export (if selected so). But I’m not sure it’s a common practice: I’ll interview customers. Yet I definitely see a use case: print & draft planning (with a good old pen).

I just see that your gantt keeps the items order from the main board where Monday’s gantt re-order items based on their timeline (which what we would expect). Yet I think your way is better, more flexible, and would justify to see the unplanned element at their current position (from the main board).

Allowing to re-organize items order from your gantt view would be a nice-to-have. But switching to the board view to adapt item order is not a big deal.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Hanael Great points! the board order is intentional- it gives your more control over how things look, especially for exports. And yes, reorganize items directly from the gantt view is on the list!

I’ll look into showing unscheduled items inline within their groups too- make sense for the print&draft use case. Let me know what your customers say!

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Hanael, we have added most features you asked for:

  • Toggle for progress percentage on chart
  • Custom footer for exports
  • Custom notes in exports
  • Export date toggle
  • Column picker for pdf exports

They are all live now. Let me know if you miss anything else!

posts.author: sophiasmith (Sophia Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re not alone here, the current Gantt export really does behave like a single screenshot, so it’s tough to get a clean multi page printout. I ended up doing a workaround by adjusting the zoom and taking sections into separate PDFs, but it’s definitely not ideal.

posts.author: Hanael (Hanael)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sweet! So I guess I must start my part of the deal…

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hahaha Indeed you should!
